sourceRefs.summary: Worker C dataset inventory from PDFs, selected supplements, GEO, Nature, and GitHub.
Worker C missing data and unconfirmed item report.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper reports de novo H3K4me3 peaks and DSB hotspot overlap/correlation.
Downloaded Supplementary Table S3 contains de novo H3K4me3 peak lists by stage.

methodRefs.summary: ChIP-seq processing, peak calling, de novo/common H3K4me3 classification, heatmaps, and statistics methods.

codeRefs.summary: Original source code is request-only; reimplementation required.

rows.rationale: Primary and external datasets plus peak tables are located, but no data materialization or computational overlap/correlation check has been run in this pass.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper reports PRDM9-mediated H3K4me3 appears in leptotene, peaks in mid-zygotene, persists into pachytene, and nearly disappears later.
Stage-resolved de novo H3K4me3 peak tables can support a direct count check.

methodRefs.summary: Peak calling, bedGraph/bigWig normalization, and heatmap/profile procedures.

rows.rationale: The supplement provides countable peak tables, but this worker did not parse the tables into a reproduced stage-count figure.

evidenceRefs.summary: Abstract claims earlier formed DSBs occupy more open chromatin and are more competent to proceed to CO fate.
Results connect H3K4me3 width/density, PRDM9 binding, NDRs, and fate interpretation.

methodRefs.summary: Methods define H3K4me3 peak subtypes and profile calculations.
Methods define NDR detection from NOMe-seq GCH methylation.

rows.rationale: The paper provides a mechanistic/fate interpretation, but this pass has not checked CO/NCO proxy data, genetic-map linkage, or NOMe accessibility statistics. This is a data/analysis gap, not a contradiction.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper section states open chromatin states contribute to DSB fate decision, with NDR and GCH accessibility analyses.

methodRefs.summary: NOMe-seq library prep, Bismark processing, GCH/WCG methylation calls, and NDR definition.

rows.rationale: NOMe-seq data are discoverable, but the accessibility/NDR computations were not rerun and the original source code is not public.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper reports up to 5136 broad H3K4me3 domains in spermatids, with promoter and distal enhancer distribution.
Downloaded Supplementary Table S5 contains broad H3K4me3 and broad H3K27ac domains.

methodRefs.summary: Broad H3K4me3/H3K27ac domains defined by bedtools merge -d -500 and length greater than 5 kb.

rows.rationale: Public data, supplement domain table, and a public ChIP-seq pipeline are located, but domain calling was not re-executed.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper reports broad H3K4me3 domains coincide with more than 76 percent of broad H3K27ac peaks.
Broad H3K4me3 and H3K27ac domain tables downloaded.

methodRefs.summary: Domain definition and promoter/distal classification method.

rows.rationale: Overlap is directly testable from downloaded Supplementary Table S5 or processed BED files, but no overlap computation was run.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper reports Setd1b cKO strongly depletes broad H3K4me3 and affects transcriptional output and Pol II occupancy.

methodRefs.summary: Methods cover RNA-seq, ChIP-seq, broad-domain definitions, and statistics.

rows.rationale: Perturbation data are likely in GSE242515, but this pass did not separate control/cKO samples or rerun differential broad-domain and expression analyses. Public RNA/NOMe source code is incomplete.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper discusses early and late spermiogenesis gene timing changes.
Downloaded Supplementary Table S8 contains early and late expressed gene sets.

rows.rationale: Temporal-expression claim requires reanalysis of RNA-seq or at least parsing Supplementary Table S8. This pass only located the data and table.

evidenceRefs.summary: Paper reports RFX2 motif/KO evidence and RFX2-SETD1B interaction evidence.

methodRefs.summary: Motif/domain/enhancer assignment methods relevant to RFX2 axis checks.

rows.rationale: A mechanistic axis needs motif enrichment, KO sample comparison, and interaction evidence; this pass located data/code clues but did not run those checks.

diagnostics: All verdicts are deliberately not-tested or insufficient-evidence because this worker performed discovery and inventory only.
No row should be interpreted as reproduced, partially-reproduced, not-reproduced, or contradicted until a downstream execution artifact supplies computed outputs and statistics.
